subject: [PATCH v2 047/110] ufs: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the PRIino format being used in various ufs i_ino format strings. The author's response is neutral, providing an explanation of why the PRIino format is necessary for future widening of i_ino via kino_t and stating that they will not revert to the old (unsigned long) casts on i_ino.

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch converts the UFS file system to use the PRIino format macro for printing inode numbers (i_ino) in preparation for widening i_ino via kino_t. The PRIino format is used instead of explicit casts to unsigned long, which are no longer necessary. This change affects several files within the UFS codebase and ensures consistency in how inode numbers are printed.
